success_surface: Correctly aggregated material area calculations and wrote summaries to target cells while preserving workbook integrity
effective_behaviors: Used openpyxl to load workbooks when needing to preserve cell values and avoid formula conversion
Parsed target output structure first to establish which rows/materials should receive updates
Normalized string keys (material names) for consistent aggregation across case variations
Validated cell values before processing (skip None, handle type conversion safely)
Grouped and aggregated calculations outside of sheet-write operations for efficiency
Verified changes post-save by re-reading the output file
Only updated specified target cells rather than rewriting entire sheets
Used debug prints for intermediate validation without blocking execution
evidence: Agent loaded workbook with openpyxl.load_workbook() rather than pandas.read_excel()
Read material names from column G rows 2-4 BEFORE writing totals to column H
Applied str().lower() normalization on material names before grouping
Printed calculated material totals and verification readback before saving
Wrote only to specific target cells (ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=8).value) instead of df.to_excel()
Confirmed H2:H4 contained correct totals (glass: 3710.0, metal: 1660.0, pvc: 2753.0)

memory_items.title: Workbook Preservation via OpenPyXL
memory_items.lesson: When formulas, formatting, or mixed cell content must be preserved, use openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas.read_excel()
memory_items.generalization: For tasks requiring selective cell updates without losing structural elements (formulas, styles, merged cells), prefer openpyxl Workbook objects over pandas DataFrame-only workflows

memory_items.title: Structure Discovery Before Modification
memory_items.lesson: Read the target output region (headers, labels, expected row count) before writing calculated results
memory_items.generalization: Always inspect the destination worksheet layout to understand which rows/columns need updates and maintain alignment with existing data labels

memory_items.title: String Normalization for Aggregation
memory_items.lesson: Normalize text-based grouping keys (lowercase, strip whitespace) to ensure consistent matching
memory_items.generalization: When aggregating by categorical string values, apply consistent normalization (case-folding, trimming) to prevent duplicate groupings from minor format differences

memory_items.title: Selective Cell Writes Over Full-Sheet Replacement
memory_items.lesson: Modify only the required target cells rather than using pandas.to_excel() which replaces the entire sheet
memory_items.generalization: Use openpyxl worksheet.cell() assignment for targeted updates when preserving other regions is critical; reserve to_excel() for complete rebuilds

memory_items.title: Post-Save Verification
memory_items.lesson: Re-load and inspect the saved file to confirm changes applied correctly
memory_items.generalization: After saving modifications, perform a sanity-check readback to verify data integrity, especially for critical output cells

edits.content:
## Best Practices from Successful Trajectories

### Workbook Preservation
- When formulas, cell formats, or mixed content matter, prefer `openpyxl.load_workbook()` over `pandas.read_excel()`
- Use `to_excel()` only when rebuilding a sheet from scratch; use `worksheet.cell()` for targeted edits

### Target Structure First
- Always inspect destination region (headers, labels, row count) before writing aggregated results
- Align write operations with existing labels to maintain consistency

### Data Aggregation Safeguards
- Normalize string keys (lowercase, trim) before grouping to prevent duplicate categories
- Wrap numeric conversions in try/except blocks for robustness
- Aggregate outside the worksheet write loop for performance

### Verification Workflow
- Print intermediate calculations for debugging
- Re-read saved file to confirm critical cells updated correctly
- Compare output against expected schema before final submission
